text: Patient presents for routine preventive visit (physical, labs) after a couple of years; encouraged by brother
transcript_quote: The chart says you are due for the works — physical, labs, the whole menu.

text: Patient reports no chief complaint or bothersome symptoms
transcript_quote: Not really. I am fine.

text: Chronic difficulty sleeping; has never slept easily
transcript_quote: I have never slept easy, so I take the Benadryl

text: Morning grogginess associated with nightly diphenhydramine use, relieved by coffee
transcript_quote: I am groggy some mornings, but coffee fixes it.

text: Diphenhydramine (Benadryl) 25 mg oral nightly for sleep — plan to demote to bad-nights-only use

slots.freq: nightly (to reduce to as-needed for bad nights)

transcript_quote: I take the Benadryl — the pink twenty-five-milligram ones. Most nights.

text: Counseled on sleep hygiene: consistent bedtime, screens off, no caffeine after noon, no working from bed
transcript_quote: same bedtime every night, screens off, no caffeine after noon, and the dispatch chair not doubling as a bed

text: Alcohol use: a couple of beers about two nights a week; AUDIT-C score of 2 (low risk)
transcript_quote: the alcohol score came to a two, low-risk territory

text: Former smoker; quit at age 20, over 30 years ago
transcript_quote: Quit at twenty. Have not touched one in over thirty years.

text: PHQ-2 depression screen score of 0 (negative)
transcript_quote: Zero on the depression screen both times

text: GAD-7 anxiety screen score of 2 (minimal/normal)
transcript_quote: a two on the anxiety scale, which is nothing

text: HARK domestic abuse/safety screen score of 0; feels safe at home
transcript_quote: the safety-at-home one — all came back clean

text: Pain severity score 0/10; no pain anywhere
transcript_quote: Zero. Nothing hurts.

text: Social isolation identified as a health risk; patient lives with brother, keeps to himself, reports occasional loneliness
transcript_quote: isolation sits on my list of health risks, same as cholesterol

text: Counseled to establish one standing social activity per week (e.g., watching games with brother)
transcript_quote: One standing thing a week. Coffee with the brother counts.

text: Lives with brother; two people in household; full-time work as home-office dispatcher
transcript_quote: just the two of us

text: History of incarceration: spent more than 2 nights in jail/prison in past year per PRAPARE
transcript_quote: After everything with my record, people made themselves scarce

text: Has stable housing, not worried about losing it; feels safe at home; has private insurance; annual family income ~$143,750
Oral exam: inflamed, red, puffy lower gums that bled on light touch; gingivitis diagnosed
transcript_quote: Your gums are inflamed along the bottom — red, puffy, and they bled just now with a light touch. That is gingivitis.

text: Patient reports gums bleed a little with brushing; last dental visit ~10 years ago
transcript_quote: A little. I figured that was normal at my age.

text: Referral to dental care placed for gingivitis evaluation/cleaning
transcript_quote: the referral goes in today

text: Counseled that gum inflammation is early-stage, reversible with cleaning and flossing; noted association between gum health and heart health
transcript_quote: gum inflammation and heart health are connected, which is one more reason we treat it

text: Physical exam: heart regular, lungs clear, abdomen soft, no leg edema
transcript_quote: heart is regular, lungs clear, belly soft, no swelling in the legs

text: Elevated blood pressure 143/75 mmHg in office
transcript_quote: Your blood pressure today is one forty-three over seventy-five. That top number is higher than I want to see.

text: Ordered home blood pressure monitoring twice daily (morning and evening) for two weeks with recheck visit
transcript_quote: a pharmacy cuff, morning and evening, write them down for two weeks, and we recheck here

text: Heart rate 75/min; respiratory rate 14/min
Lipid panel excellent: total cholesterol 115 mg/dL, LDL 30 mg/dL, HDL 56 mg/dL, triglycerides 142 mg/dL
transcript_quote: Cholesterol is genuinely excellent — total one-fifteen, the bad LDL down at thirty, the good HDL fifty-six, triglycerides one forty-two.

text: CBC normal: hemoglobin 17.4 g/dL, platelets 318 x10^3/uL, WBC 7.8 x10^3/uL
transcript_quote: Blood counts all normal — hemoglobin seventeen point four, platelets three-eighteen, white count seven point eight.

text: BMI 30.0 (weight 98.1 kg, height 180.6 cm) — obesity
transcript_quote: Ninety-eight kilos at your height puts the BMI right at thirty

text: Sedentary lifestyle; minimal physical activity during workday
transcript_quote: The walk from the desk to the fridge.

text: Counseled to walk 30 minutes after work, 5 days/week (reservoir loop) for weight, BP, sleep, and social benefit
transcript_quote: Thirty minutes of walking after the shift ends, five days a week.

text: Influenza vaccine (split virus, trivalent, preservative-free) administered today
transcript_quote: Last item — flu shot. You are due

text: Follow-up: BP recheck visit in a few weeks with home readings
transcript_quote: See you in a few weeks with that spreadsheet

text: Health and social care needs assessment completed
